stories.title: 'Continuity Keir': Burnham keeping on many of Starmer's No. 10 advisers
stories.summary: Andy Burnham is retaining many of Keir Starmer's key Downing Street advisers, raising concerns among some Labour supporters that next week's policy announcements may lack the radicalism they had hoped for.

stories.title: Keir Starmer makes Sadiq Khan a peer in the House of Lords
stories.summary: Outgoing Prime Minister Keir Starmer has awarded Sadiq Khan a peerage in his resignation honours list, potentially opening the door for the London mayor to join Andy Burnham's cabinet.

stories.title: Andy Burnham to promise 'new path' when he becomes Prime Minister
stories.summary: Andy Burnham will be confirmed as Labour leader at a special party conference in London before taking over from Sir Keir Starmer as Prime Minister. He is expected to signal a new direction for his government.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, Lords, PMQs & Legislation

stories.title: House of Commons sits on 16 July — Business of the House debate
stories.summary: The House of Commons held its regular Business of the House debate on 16 July 2026, with the Backbench Business Committee determining the weekly schedule. Upcoming business includes remaining stages of the Representation of the People Bill.

stories.title: Commons Chamber debate: Local Government Reorganisation and NHS
stories.summary: MPs debated local government reorganisation and raised urgent questions including calls for a new North Tees hospital. Reform UK MPs were noted as 'not interested in fixing local issues' during exchanges.

stories.title: Representation of the People Bill: remaining stages scheduled
stories.summary: The Representation of the People Bill has been scheduled for its remaining Commons stages in September, signalling continued parliamentary focus on electoral reform legislation.

topics.name: Labour Party
topics.description: Labour Party news, policy & internal politics

stories.title: Andy Burnham to promise 'fix the big things' in first speech as Labour leader
stories.summary: Incoming PM Andy Burnham will address a special Labour conference in London to be confirmed as party leader, pledging to 'fix the big things' and chart a course distinct from Starmer's tenure.

stories.title: Burnham 'too influenced by vested interests' to offer real change, Greens say
stories.summary: Green Party co-leader Zack Polanski has challenged the notion that Andy Burnham will pursue a more left-leaning agenda than Starmer, accusing the incoming PM of being too close to vested interests.

stories.title: From Cambridge 'impostor' to New Labour star: Andy Burnham's winding path to power
stories.summary: A two-part Guardian profile traces Burnham's early political career and his rise from New Labour backbencher to becoming the incoming Prime Minister.

stories.title: Liz Truss assembles rightwing speakers for UK's own CPAC event in London
stories.summary: Former PM Liz Truss has organised a UK version of the US Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in London, with keynote speakers including US influencer Jack Posobiec, as she aims to build influence on the British right.

stories.title: Badenoch: Burnham must back oil and gas drilling 'if he has any common sense'
stories.summary: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch has called on incoming PM Andy Burnham to reverse Labour's North Sea drilling restrictions, warning that abandoning fossil fuels prematurely would harm the economy.

stories.title: Burnham planning summer 'listening tour' of UK in early weeks as PM
stories.summary: Andy Burnham plans to travel across the UK during August while MPs are in summer recess, in what is being described as a 'listening tour' — a move that has drawn mixed reactions from Conservatives.

stories.title: Five new Liberal Democrat peers created in Starmer's resignation honours
stories.summary: Keir Starmer's resignation honours list includes five new Liberal Democrat peers, alongside the more prominent appointments of Sadiq Khan and broadcaster June Sarpong.

stories.title: Ed Davey calls for Argentina World Cup players to be suspended over Falklands banner
stories.summary: Lib Dem leader Ed Davey has called for Argentina players who held a Falklands-related banner during the World Cup to be suspended by FIFA ahead of Sunday's final against Spain.

stories.title: Liz Truss hosts CPAC-style event attracting figures from Reform UK orbit
stories.summary: A UK Conservative Political Action Conference organised by Liz Truss drew speakers from the broader British right including figures associated with Reform UK's political space, reflecting ongoing competition on the right of British politics.

stories.title: Reform UK MPs attack government over failure to address local priorities
stories.summary: During Commons Chamber exchanges on 16 July, Reform UK was criticised for being 'not interested in fixing the issues that local people care about', pointing to ongoing tensions between the party's national messaging and local governance.

stories.title: Suella Braverman column on reparations and colonialism draws backlash
stories.summary: A Guardian commentary challenges Suella Braverman's argument that former colonies should pay reparations to former colonisers, describing the position — gaining traction in Reform-adjacent circles — as having 'the telescope the wrong way round'.

stories.title: Sturgeon 'stitched up' Salmond to cover up Murrell fraud, says senior Tory MP
stories.summary: Senior Conservative MP David Davis has accused former SNP leader Nicola Sturgeon of lying about her knowledge of her husband Peter Murrell's embezzlement, claiming she orchestrated a cover-up.

stories.title: Starmer's final foreign trip: Ukraine visit before handing over to Burnham
stories.summary: Outgoing PM Keir Starmer made a final visit to Ukraine to meet President Zelensky before leaving office, a trip covered extensively in Scottish media amid the broader transition of power.

stories.title: Starmer creates 26 new peers — including Sadiq Khan, June Sarpong and Christina McAnea
stories.summary: Outgoing PM Keir Starmer has announced 26 new life peerages in his resignation honours list, including London Mayor Sadiq Khan, broadcaster June Sarpong, and former union chief Christina McAnea.

stories.title: Chancellor announces steps toward Digital Gilt Instrument (DIGIT) pilot expansion
stories.summary: Following the Chancellor's Mansion House speech on 14 July, the government has published an update on the Digital Gilt Instrument pilot, setting out steps to prepare for potential further issuances.

stories.title: JCVI publishes new recommendation on adolescent MenB vaccination
stories.summary: The Joint Committee on Vaccination and Immunisation (JCVI) has published a recommendation to the Government on expanding MenB vaccination to adolescents; the Department of Health and Social Care will now consider the advice.

stories.title: Representation of the People Bill: remaining stages scheduled for September
stories.summary: The Representation of the People Bill — a key piece of electoral reform legislation — has been scheduled for its remaining Commons stages in September 2026, keeping electoral reform on the parliamentary agenda.

stories.title: Burnham confirmed as Labour leader at special party conference — becomes PM
stories.summary: Andy Burnham's formal election as Labour leader at a special London conference marks the end of Starmer's leadership and triggers the transfer of power; the process highlights Labour's internal electoral mechanism for leadership succession.

stories.title: Sadiq Khan among 26 new peers created by Starmer in resignation honours
stories.summary: London Mayor Sadiq Khan heads the list of 26 new life peers created by outgoing PM Keir Starmer. The appointments also include broadcaster June Sarpong and former TUC general secretary Christina McAnea.

stories.title: Keir Starmer makes Sadiq Khan a peer — potentially for Burnham cabinet role
stories.summary: Starmer's decision to elevate Sadiq Khan to the Lords is seen as a strategic move that could allow the former London Mayor to join Andy Burnham's cabinet in a Lords-based ministerial role.

stories.title: Five new Liberal Democrat and three new Conservative peers among Starmer's 26 appointments
stories.summary: Alongside high-profile Labour appointments, Starmer's resignation honours list includes five Liberal Democrat peers, three Conservatives, and two cross-bench members, reflecting a cross-party tradition in Lords appointments.
